think-cell Slide
Project IGNITE Current State Assessment
PowerPoint Presentation
3 big misconceptions initially masked the real challenges
HGV’s compensation levels and plan components vary from market standards in 4 key areas
We identified 10 operating model gaps spread across compensation design, employee experience, governance, and supporting technology
Compensation sentiment was captured throughout the organization across all compensable roles, regions, and seniority levels
Employee survey reflects overall compensation satisfaction, with opportunities to improve compensation fairness, quota alignment, and lead quality
Sales employee sentiment on compensation-related topics is notably more negative than Marketing and Telemarketing
HGV needs to align on the quick wins and long-term vision by balancing benefit, cost and time to execute
PowerPoint Presentation
Understanding the compensation program differential vs the market
April 14 SteerCo Agenda & Objectives
PwC conducted interviews with HGV and reviewed your data to understand the current state, uncover pain points, and compare to competitors
PowerPoint Presentation
3 big misconceptions initially masked the real challenges
HGV’s compensation levels and plan components vary from market standards in 4 key areas
We identified 10 operating model gaps spread across compensation design, employee experience, governance, and supporting technology
Gaps discovered in the assessment reveal causality to challenges experienced today across several business functions at HGV
Compensation design findings highlight where HGV deviates from market best practices
Plan proliferation drives inconsistency in incentives, weakens transparency, and limits enterprise-wide alignment
Compensation sentiment was captured throughout the organization across all compensable roles, regions, and seniority levels
Employee survey reflects overall compensation satisfaction, with opportunities to improve compensation fairness, quota alignment, and lead quality
Sales employee sentiment on compensation-related topics is notably more negative than Marketing and Telemarketing
Bluegreen employee sentiment is consistently more positive than legacy HGV, though they still see room for improvement on compensation and lead quality
HGV has opportunities to increase talent attraction and retention through enhanced employee value programs and career pathing
Disparate brands, no formal governance, and lack of compensation philosophy create fragmentation, undermine trust, and prevent growth
Varicent’s full ICM capabilities won’t be realized based on the current roadmap; business objectives and data requirements must be revisited
We propose rapid design for a handful of quick win opportunities while planning for long-term vision in parallel
HGV needs to align on the quick wins and long-term vision by balancing benefit, cost and time to execute
PowerPoint Presentation
PwC conducted an external benchmarking assessment to understand your current state, uncover pain points, and compare to competitors
Our compensation design assessment was guided by an 8-point design framework
Each dimension of the compensation design framework presents opportunity to compare HGV against leading practices
The overall assessment unveiled several key gaps across compensation levels and design

PowerPoint Presentation
PowerPoint Presentation
Current-state governance gaps limit alignment and scalability, requiring a more structured, enterprise model
Maturity assessment of foundational barriers across governance structure
Maturity assessment of foundational barriers across planning & process
A fragmented and manual target setting process limits agility, accuracy and visibility
Maturity assessment of foundational barriers across tools & data
Reliance on fragmented source data and manual files by site and job, in tandem with insufficient governance controls undermines control and scale
PowerPoint Presentation
Leadership largely agrees on the problems, but diverges on the mechanisms to solve them
What we heard, what we observed, and what it means for HGV’s incentive transformation
Leadership alignment varies across 5 key pillars

Sales employee sentiment on compensation-related topics is notably more negative than Marketing and Telemarketing
Bluegreen employee sentiment is consistently more positive than legacy HGV, though they still see room for improvement on compensation and lead quality
Seller Voice Summary: Key Themes from Open Feedback

Telemarketing Response Heatmap (2 of 2)
Marketing Response Heatmap (1 of 2)
Marketing Response Heatmap (2 of 2)
Sales Response Heatmap (1 of 2)
Sales Response Heatmap (2 of 2)
Q1: My role and responsibilities are clearly defined.
Q1: My role and responsibilities are clearly defined.
Q2: I know what strong performance looks like in my position.
Q2: I know what strong performance looks like in my position.
Q3: I understand how my role contributes to the overall sales process.
Q3: I understand how my role contributes to the overall sales process.
Q4: I can describe the value proposition to my customer.
Q4: I can describe the value proposition to my customer.
Q5: My compensation reflects the effort I put in.
Q5: My compensation reflects the effort I put in.
Q6: I am comfortable with my mix of commissions vs. bonus pay.
Q6: I am comfortable with my mix of commissions vs. bonus pay.
Q7: My earnings potential is competitive with other companies in my industry.
Q7: My earnings potential is competitive with other companies in my industry.
Q8: My compensation plan encourages the right behaviors.
Q8: My compensation plan encourages the right behaviors.
Q10: I understand how my compensation and incentives are structured.
Q10: I understand how my compensation and incentives are structured.
Q11: My compensation plan motivates me to perform at a high level.
Q11: My compensation plan motivates me to perform at a high level.
Q12: My compensation plan document clearly communicates plan details.
Q12: My compensation plan document clearly communicates plan details.
Q13: My quota target is fair and aligns with my assigned leads or territory.
Q13: My quota target is fair and aligns with my assigned leads or territory.
Q14: I can influence the outcomes that my performance is measured and paid on.
Q14: I can influence the outcomes that my performance is measured and paid on.
Q15: The metrics in my plan align with the organization's sales strategy.
Q15: The metrics in my plan align with the organization's sales strategy.
Q16: My assigned rotation gives me a fair opportunity to perform well.
Q16: My assigned rotation gives me a fair opportunity to perform well.
Q17: I am selling high quality products that allow me to be successful.
Q17: I am selling high quality products that allow me to be successful.
Q18: I am provided the resources and support to perform at a high level.
Q18: I am provided the resources and support to perform at a high level.
Q20: I receive adequate training to perform my job at a high level.
Q20: I receive adequate training to perform my job at a high level.
Q21: I prefer to receive training in the following formats.
Q22: The leads I receive are high quality.
Q22: The leads I receive are high quality.
Q23: I have the technology and tools I need to perform my job effectively.
Q23: I have the technology and tools I need to perform my job effectively.
Q24: I am proud to work in the vacation ownership industry.
Q24: I am proud to work in the vacation ownership industry.
Q25: I am proud to work at HGV.
Q25: I am proud to work at HGV.
Q26: I believe HGV is the premier company in the vacation ownership industry.
Q26: I believe HGV is the premier company in the vacation ownership industry.
